summary: webservices.rest-utils exfiltrates installer host, cloud, and CI/CD credentials to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: package.json declares preinstall, postinstall, and prepare lifecycle hooks all running `node index.js`, and the package additionally registers `bin` entries that shadow the well-known dev-tool names webpack-cli, vite, ts-node, eslint, jest, gulp, tsc, and next so every invocation of those commands also runs index.js. On execution, index.js performs a 60-second sleep then collects host hostname, username, arch, cwd, OS uptime, local IPs, /etc/resolv.conf, process list,.git/HEAD, UID/GID, ~40 named CI/CD environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_ACTOR, GITLAB_*, CIRCLE_*, BUILDKITE_*, TRAVIS_*, VERCEL_*, etc.), nearby package.json metadata, and a presence map of installer secret stores (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.kube, ~/.docker, ~/.npm, ~/.npmrc, ~/.cargo, ~/.ngrok, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.bash_history). When cloud env hints are present it actively requests AWS IMDSv2, Azure, and GCP instance-metadata tokens (the AWS IMDS IP 169.254.169.254 is hidden as the integer string '2852039166', which https.get accepts as a hostname) and includes the first 40 chars of the AWS session Token in the payload. Collected data is base64-chunked into GET request paths to https://openmrs-sol-dev-v2.lapxa354.workers.dev/, whose hostname is base64-obfuscated in the source. The combination of install-time and bin-hijack triggers, IMDS credential theft, CI/CD env scrape, secret-store profiling, runtime-decoded C2 hostname, and integer-encoded IMDS IP is an unambiguous credential-harvest supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
